truthAlignment.canonicalForRuntimeCandidateGates: Use the live promotion gates and restored runtime source as candidate gate authority until the project explicitly migrates them to the measured reference-execution/setpiece order.

staleOrDiagnostic.read: They agree with each other and should guide the next truth-migration discussion.

staleOrDiagnostic.read: They currently preserve the restored baseline order and block candidate source promotion when a projection differs.

sources.canonicality: measured setpiece contract; agrees with reference execution description
live gate for runtime promotion until explicitly migrated

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: spawnChallenge resolves each enemy pathFamily from motionSpecGroup.pathFamilyHint before layout.groupPathFamilies; check-challenge-motion-profile validates runtime, layout, and contract order.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: This is label and gate alignment only. It must not claim object-track score lift unless paired with a runtime-expressible movement transform.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: spawn offsets, phase durations, and reference path timing are consumed by browser runtime, but the current semantic candidate only emits visibleStartS/visibleEndS trial vectors.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: The trial score can improve by assigning visible windows directly. That is optimistic unless the compiler proves which consumed runtime fields produce those windows.
runtimeExpressibility.remainingCompilerGap: Compile visibleStartS/visibleEndS intent into explicit spawnOffsetS, phaseDurations, reference duration, or playbackScale changes and verify actual browser-visible windows.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: No runtime source field changes; this class carries shot-opportunity evidence as an invariant.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: Cannot make a candidate source-ready by itself.

runtimeExpressibility.runtimeConsumer: No runtime source field changes unless another transform touches groups 4 or 5; this class preserves measured group floors.
runtimeExpressibility.predictionLimits: A predictedRuntimeVector used only for protection is not proof that a source edit can express a new vector.

overpredictedTransforms.read: Path-family labels transferred while applied, but label alignment alone did not move object-track score and conflicted with live motion/profile order.
Predicted lift came from visibleStartS/visibleEndS trial vectors, but the runtime projection did not compile those windows into consumed timing fields.
Guardrail held; this class does not claim movement lift.
Group 5 was preserved and group 4 did not regress in actual runtime, but this protection is not a movement compiler.

likelyCauses.read: phase-duration-rebalance emitted visible windows but no consumed runtime timing fields.
reference execution and setpiece orders disagree with the restored live motion-profile/target-contract/runtime order.
the trial evaluator scored optimistic vectors without browser runtime proof.
the runtime edit projected path-family labels but not the phase-window movement that produced the predicted score lift.

decision.nextRecommendation: Do not try another Stage 7 runtime candidate. First reconcile the Stage 7 path-family source of truth or make the batch gate deliberately read a single canonical artifact, then compile phase-duration intent into explicit consumed runtime controls and prove the mapping with browser conformance and motion/profile checks.
decision.remainingCompilerGap: phase-duration-rebalance has no runtime-expressible mapping from visibleStartS/visibleEndS to consumed spawnOffsetS, phaseDurations, reference duration, or playbackScale fields.
